data.description:

AMENDMENT

FORM 8.5 (EPT/RI)

PUBLIC DEALING DISCLOSURE BY AN EXEMPT PRINCIPAL TRADER WITH RECOGNISED INTERMEDIARY STATUS DEALING IN A CLIENT-SERVING CAPACITY

3.         OTHER INFORMATION

(a)        Indemnity and other dealing arrangements

  • Details of any indemnity or option arrangement, or any agreement or understanding, formal or informal, relating to relevant securities which may be an inducement to deal or refrain from dealing entered into by the exempt principal trader making the disclosure and any party to the offer or any person acting in concert with a party to the offer:

(b)        Agreements, arrangements or understandings relating to options or derivatives

data.title: Wheat steady-up 2 cents, corn up 3-5, soybeans up 3-12 cents
data.description:
The following are U.S. expectations for the resumption of grain and soy complex trading at the Chicago Board of Trade at 8:30 a.m. CDT (1330 GMT) on Wednesday.

WHEAT - Steady to up 2 cents per bushel

* CBOT wheat futures received support from reports of a planned meeting between senior U.S. and Chinese officials, which boosted hopes that tariff tensions would ease between the world's two largest economies.

* U.S. Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent and chief trade negotiator Jamieson Greer will meet China's economic tsar He Lifeng in Switzerland this weekend for talks that could be the first step toward resolving a trade war disrupting the global economy.

* Henan province, a key wheat-producing region in China, has issued a risk warning as dry, hot winds threaten the development of its wheat crop.

* Wheat futures were pressured by a firming U.S. dollar, which rose against other major currencies as investors looked towards the outcome of the Federal Reserve policy meeting and tariff negotiations.

* CBOT July soft red winter wheat (WN25) was last up 1-3/4 cents at $5.37-3/4 per bushel. K.C. July hard red winter wheat (KWN25) was last down 3 cents at $5.35 a bushel, while Minneapolis July spring wheat (MWEN25) was last down 2-1/2 cents to $6.08 a bushel.

CORN - Up 3 to 5 cents per bushel

* Corn futures headed up as traders hoped news of negotiations between senior U.S. and Chinese officials could begin to resolve the trade war causing chaos in the global economy.

* Favorable weather over the next 10 days in the U.S. Midwest is expected to provide seeding opportunities, according to Commodity Weather Group.

* South Korea's Major Feedmill Group (MFG) has issued an international tender to purchase up to 140,000 metric tons of animal feed corn sourced from South America or South Africa only, European traders said on Wednesday.

* Leading South Korean feedmaker Nonghyup Feed Inc. (NOFI) purchased around 66,000 metric tons of animal feed corn in a private deal on Wednesday without an international tender being issued, European traders said.

* CBOT July corn (CN25) was last up 4-1/2 cents to $4.60 per bushel.

SOYBEANS - Up 3 to 12 cents per bushel

* Soybean futures rallied as news of talks between senior U.S. and Chinese officials caused optimism among investors that trade tensions surrounding U.S. tariff policy might ease.

* Dry weather in parts of the U.S. Midwest is expected to bring seeding opportunities, according to Commodity Weather Group.

* CBOT July soybeans (SN25) were last up 10-1/2 cents at $10.51-3/4 per bushel.

data.title: China Warns to Push Back Against "Infiltration And Sabotage Activities" Over CIA Spy Recruitment Videos
data.description:
China said it will take action to "push back infiltration and sabotage activities from overseas" after the Central Intelligence Agency released videos recruiting Chinese officials to leak information to the US, a Chinese foreign ministry spokesperson said in a press conference on Tuesday.

Spokesperson Lin Jian said the videos, which were released on social media, violate international law and are a "serious infringement on China's national interest and pure political provocation".

The CIA started recruiting new informants from China, Iran and North Korea as part of a drive launched in October 2024, posting instructions online on how to reach out to the agency, Reuters reported on the same day.

The Chinese state security ministry recently announced the arrest of a government employee stealing state secrets to a foreign intelligence agency, the report said.

data.title: Sector Update: Financial Stocks Edge Higher Premarket Wednesday
data.description:
Financial stocks edged higher premarket Wednesday with the Financial Select Sector SPDR Fund advancing by 0.3% recently.

The Direxion Daily Financial Bull 3X Shares was up 1% and its bearish counterpart Direxion Daily Financial Bear 3X Shares was 0.8% lower.

Payoneer Global shares fell by over 6% after the company reported a decline in Q1 earnings and suspended its 2025 guidance, citing "current macroeconomic uncertainty."

MarketAxess Holdings shares were down 0.8% after the company reported lower Q1 earnings and revenue.

Blackstone Secured Lending Fund shares were up 0.9% after the company reported lower Q1 net income and total investment income that increased year-over-year.

data.title: Immunocore Holdings' Q1 Swings to Net Income, Revenue Rises
data.description:
Immunocore Holdings reported Q1 net income Wednesday of $0.10 per diluted share, swinging from a loss of $0.49 a year earlier.

Analysts polled by FactSet expected a loss of $0.38.

Revenue for the quarter ended March 31 was $93.9 million, up from $70.5 million a year earlier.

Analysts surveyed by FactSet expected $86.5 million.

The company said that as of March 31, it has $837 million in cash, cash equivalents and marketable securities.

Shares of the company were up 2.6% in recent Wednesday premarket activity.

data.title: Innovative Pharmaceutical Biotech Signs MoU with Blockchain Firm
data.description:
Innovative Pharmaceutical Biotech entered into a memorandum of understanding with Conflux Hong Kong Management to cooperate on the infrastructure and common key technologies related to the Conflux blockchain, a Tuesday Hong Kong bourse filing said.

The cooperation scope includes the research and development of innovative technologies, and exploration of application scenarios with possible equity cooperation.

Hong Kong-incorporated Conflux engages mainly in the development of blockchain technology.

data.description: GE Healthcare Technologies has an average rating of overweight and mean price target of $86.76, according to analysts polled by FactSet.

data.title: Oil Pares Gains After Tuesday Rebound
data.description:
WTI crude oil hovered near $59 per barrel, trimming earlier losses after a 3.4% rebound the day before.

Investors are closely watching for official US stockpile data, with forecasts pointing to an 800,000-barrel decline, following a larger-than-expected 4.5 million-barrel drop reported by the API. Oil markets remain under pressure after OPEC+ announced plans to accelerate production increases, raising oversupply fears at a time when US tariffs have dampened the global demand outlook.

Despite the recent price slump to four-year lows, signs of a potential supply correction are emerging.

Some US producers, including Diamondback Energy and Coterra Energy, are responding by reducing drilling activity, which analysts believe could tighten supply and help lift prices over time.

Meanwhile, traders are also awaiting the Federal Reserve’s rate decision, with expectations that it will hold rates steady amid growing economic uncertainty linked to ongoing trade tensions.

data.title: Century Enka's Consolidated Net Profit Plummets in Fiscal Q4
data.description:
Century Enka said its net profit plunged to 67.6 million Indian rupees in the fiscal fourth quarter from 202.7 million rupees a year earlier, according to a Tuesday filing.

Earnings per share for the three months ended March 31 fell to 3.09 rupees from 9.28 rupees a year ago.

Revenue from operations of the nylon and polyester filament yarns manufacturer increased year on year to 4.44 billion rupees from 4.69 billion rupees.

The board of Century Enka declared a dividend of 10 rupees per share for the fiscal year ended March 31.

data.title: Brent Crude Oil Hovers at $62
data.description:
Brent crude oil hovered near $62 per barrel, trimming earlier losses after a 3.4% rebound the day before.

Investors are closely watching for official US stockpile data, with forecasts pointing to an 800,000-barrel decline, following a larger-than-expected 4.5 million-barrel drop reported by the API. Oil markets remain under pressure after OPEC+ announced plans to accelerate production increases, raising oversupply fears at a time when US tariffs have dampened the global demand outlook.

Despite the recent price slump to four-year lows, signs of a potential supply correction are emerging.

Some US producers, including Diamondback Energy and Coterra Energy, are responding by reducing drilling activity, which analysts believe could tighten supply and help lift prices over time.

Meanwhile, traders are also awaiting the Federal Reserve’s rate decision, with expectations that it will hold rates steady amid growing economic uncertainty linked to ongoing trade tensions.

data.title: UK Regulator Seeks Feedback on Omnicom-Interpublic Group Deal
data.description:
The UK's Competition and Markets Authority invited interested parties to comment on the potential impact of Omnicom Group's planned takeover of The Interpublic Group of Companies on competition within the British markets.

The regulator said Wednesday that the invitation is part of its initial information-gathering process, adding that it has not launched a formal investigation into the advertising companies' transaction.

The deadline for submitting feedback is May 21.

data.title: Swiss 10-Year Government Bond Yield at Over 3-Year Low
data.description:
The yield on the 10-year Swiss government bond hovered around 0.10%, its lowest since January 2022, as investors priced in expectations for lower rates.

The Swiss headline inflation rate dropped to 0% year-on-year in April, bringing it just one step away from deflation, while the core inflation rate also saw a surprisingly sharp decline.

This supported bets of another rate cut on June 19.

In the meantime, Chair Martin Schlegel indicated that the Swiss National Bank (SNB) stands ready to reduce its key interest rate even further in light of economic uncertainty, including the potential for reintroducing a zero or negative interest rate policy.

data.title: Kennametal's Fiscal Q3 Adjusted Earnings Rise, Sales Fall
data.description:
Kennametal reported fiscal Q3 adjusted earnings Wednesday of $0.47 per diluted share, up from $0.30 a year earlier.

Analysts polled by FactSet expected $0.24.

Sales for the quarter ended March 31 were $486.4 million, down from $515.8 million a year earlier.

Analysts surveyed by FactSet expected $489.3 million.

The company said it now expects fiscal 2025 adjusted EPS of $1.30 to $1.45, compared with its prior guidance of between $1.05 and $1.30. Analysts polled by FactSet expect $1.13.

Sales for the fiscal year are now expected to be $1.97 billion to $1.99 billion, compared with previous expectations of between $1.95 billion and $2 billion. Analysts surveyed by FactSet expect $1.97 billion.

Shares of the company were up 7% in recent Wednesday premarket activity.

data.title: TELUS International (CDA) Inc expected to post earnings of 5 cents a share - Earnings Preview
data.description:
• TELUS International (CDA) Inc is expected to show a fall in quarterly revenue when it reports results on May 9 for the period ending March 31 2025

  • The Vancouver British Columbia-based company is expected to report a 1.1% decrease in revenue to $650.016 million from $657 million a year ago, according to the mean estimate from 7 analysts, based on LSEG data.

  • ​LSEG's mean analyst estimate for TELUS International (CDA) Inc is for earnings of 5 cents per share.

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "hold" and the breakdown of recommendations is 2 "strong buy" or "buy," 8 "hold" and 2 "sell" or "strong sell."

  • The mean earnings estimate of analysts had risen by about 4.4% in the last three months. ​

  • Wall Street's median 12-month price target for TELUS International (CDA) Inc is $4.50​, above​ its last closing price of $2.54. ​​​

data.title: Romania's Telecredit IFN 2024 revenue soars 76%
data.description:
BUCHAREST (Romania), May 7 (SeeNews) - Romanian non-bank lender Telecredit IFN, operating under the Omnicredit brand, said on Wednesday that it has recorded revenues of 3.5 million euro ($3.97 million) in 2024, surging 76% year-on-year, as well as a net profit of 1.3 million euro.

The value of transactions financed by Telecredit IFN in 2024 increased by an annual 48% last year to 60.8 million euro, the non-bank lender said in a press release.

“We expect to maintain year-on-year growth in the value of financing at around 50% in 2025," Telecredit IFN CEO Elisa Rusu said.

Last year, Poland-based non-bank lender PragmaGO entered the Romanian market by acquiring 89% of Telecredit IFN for 5.785 million euro plus an earn-out of up to 0.445 million euro.

Telecredit IFN focuses on providing non-bank financing solutions to small and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs) through factoring and digital lending.

($ = 0.8805 euro)
